intro:
A QR code generator lets you instantly create scannable QR codes for any URL, text, email address, phone number, or other data. QR codes (Quick Response codes) are two-dimensional barcodes that smartphones can scan with their cameras to instantly open links, save contacts, connect to Wi-Fi, or access any digital content without typing.

QR codes have become ubiquitous in modern life — from restaurant menus and business cards to product packaging and event check-ins. Our free QR code generator creates high-quality codes in seconds that you can download and use anywhere. No account required, no watermarks, completely free.

Businesses use QR codes to drive traffic to websites, landing pages, and social media profiles. Individuals use them to share contact details, links, and information conveniently. Whether you need a QR code for a personal project or professional marketing material, this tool has you covered.
howItWorks: Enter the text, URL, or data you want to encode into the QR code field. Click Generate and the QR code is created instantly. You can then download the QR code as a PNG image and use it in print materials, digital presentations, websites, or anywhere else. The size and error correction level can be adjusted to balance scannability and information density.
interpretationGuide: A QR code contains encoded data readable by any QR scanner app or modern smartphone camera. Higher error correction levels make the code more resilient to damage or partial obscuring but result in a denser, more complex pattern. For most uses, the default settings produce excellent results. Test your QR code by scanning it before distributing.

faqs.question: What is a QR code?
faqs.answer: A QR code is a two-dimensional barcode that encodes data such as URLs, text, or contact information. Smartphones can scan them with the camera app to instantly access the encoded content without typing.

faqs.question: Are QR codes free to create?
faqs.answer: Yes. Our QR code generator is completely free to use. You can create unlimited QR codes without registration, and there are no watermarks or usage restrictions on the downloaded images.

faqs.question: What types of data can I encode in a QR code?
faqs.answer: You can encode URLs, plain text, email addresses, phone numbers, SMS messages, Wi-Fi credentials, vCard contact information, and more. URLs are the most common use case.

faqs.question: How long do QR codes last?
faqs.answer: A static QR code lasts indefinitely as long as the content it points to remains available. If you encode a URL, the QR code works as long as that URL stays active.

faqs.question: Can I customize the appearance of a QR code?
faqs.answer: Our generator creates standard black-and-white QR codes. For branded QR codes with logos and colors, dedicated QR branding tools offer those features, though they may charge fees.

faqs.question: What size should I make my QR code?
faqs.answer: For print materials, QR codes should be at least 2x2 cm (about 0.8 inches) for reliable scanning. Larger materials like posters should use proportionally larger codes. Digital QR codes should be at least 200x200 pixels.

faqs.question: Can I track how many times a QR code is scanned?
faqs.answer: Static QR codes cannot track scans. For tracking, use a URL shortener service that provides analytics, or use a dynamic QR code service that allows tracking and URL changes.

faqs.question: What is error correction in QR codes?
faqs.answer: Error correction allows QR codes to remain scannable even if part of the code is damaged or obscured. Higher levels (L, M, Q, H) add redundancy. Level M or Q is recommended for most uses.

faqs.question: Do all phones scan QR codes natively?
faqs.answer: Most modern smartphones (iOS 11+ and Android 9+) can scan QR codes directly with the default camera app. Older devices may need a dedicated QR scanner app.

faqs.question: Can I put a logo inside a QR code?
faqs.answer: Logos can be overlaid on QR codes because error correction preserves scannability even with part of the code obscured. The logo should cover no more than 30% of the code area for reliable scanning.
